body_txt: When Bob asks a question and the answer to the question is actually part of it. At first Bob doesn't realise this, but after a moment of reflection (expect a Beat or even a Beat Panel ) he realises that he just answered his own question. Bob will then often remark something along the lines of "Right." or "Forget I asked". Sometimes Alice will point it out to him. If Bob doesn't realize what he just did, there's a good chance that Alice will point it out anyway. The Ditz will use a more basic version of the trope, asking how to spell "ABC" or how to call 911. A variation of this trope is when Alice is discussing something, making a presumption Bob doesn't agree with. Bob will ask an either/or question, restating Alice's presumption and an alternative that would normally be reasonable. Alice and Bob will pause a moment, often burst out laughing and continue with Alice's original discussion as if nothing had happened: Differs from a rhetorical question in that Bob actually expects an answer when posing the question. Compare Is the Answer to This Question "Yes"? , Ask a Stupid Question... , Explain, Explain... Oh, Crap! .
